goal: Remove the unconditional runtime loop from the shared worker-loop primitive and add a focused contract preventing while(true) or for(;;) from returning to that primitive.

commands: git grep -n "while *(true" src tests scripts || true
git grep -n "for *(;;" src tests scripts || true
freshScanBefore.selectedRegression: src/lib/async/mapWithConcurrencyLimit.ts runtime while(true) in the newly added worker-loop primitive

reasonSelected: The only new runtime C2 finding was introduced by the shared worker-loop primitive.
Replacing the unconditional loop preserves the primitive semantics while making cancellation the loop boundary.
A focused contract test blocks unconditional loop forms in the primitive source without broadening scanner allowlists.
No concrete worker behavior is changed in this wave.

before.runtimePrimitiveLoopBoundary: while (true) with an early abort return inside the loop body
before.c2InventoryImpact: src/lib/async/mapWithConcurrencyLimit.ts appeared as a fresh runtime while(true) finding

after.runtimePrimitiveLoopBoundary: while (!isAbortStop(signal)) with an explicit aborted summary after loop exit
after.abortSemantics: unchanged: abort exits with reason aborted
after.errorSemantics: unchanged: fatal errors still throw by default and transient errors follow the onError decision
after.backoffSemantics: unchanged: continuing iterations still sleep with at least MIN_WORKER_LOOP_BACKOFF_MS

commands: git grep -n "while *(true" src tests scripts || true
git grep -n "for *(;;" src tests scripts || true

gateEvidence.focusedTests: npx jest tests/async/cancellableWorkerLoop.test.ts src/lib/async/mapWithConcurrencyLimit.test.ts src/workers/queueWorker.boundary.test.ts tests/workers/queueRunnerLifecycle.contract.test.ts tests/perf/performance-budget.test.ts --runInBand; 5 suites passed, 34 tests passed
gateEvidence.fullJestRunInBand: npm test -- --runInBand; 662 suites passed, 1 skipped, 3925 tests passed, 1 skipped
